explanation:
Tailwind CSS is a utility-first framework — instead of writing custom CSS classes, you compose small, single-purpose utility classes directly in your HTML. `text-blue-500`, `p-4`, `rounded-lg`, and `shadow-md` each do one thing. This eliminates the need to invent class names, switch between HTML and CSS files, and deal with specificity conflicts.

The initial reaction is often "this looks messy," but utility classes are actually more maintainable: changes are localized to the component, dead CSS elimination is automatic (unused classes are purged), and the design system is enforced by the framework's consistent spacing and color scales.
key_takeaway: Tailwind replaces custom CSS with composable utility classes. The `content` array in config enables tree-shaking — unused classes are purged from production builds automatically.
try_it: Convert a component from traditional CSS (custom classes) to Tailwind utilities. Compare the file size and number of CSS rules in the final build.

explanation:
Tailwind provides flexbox and grid utilities that map directly to CSS properties. `flex`, `flex-col`, `items-center`, `justify-between` create flex layouts. `grid`, `grid-cols-3`, `gap-4` create grid layouts. The utility names follow a predictable pattern, making them easy to learn and remember.

Responsive prefixes (`sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`, `xl:`) apply utilities only at specific breakpoints. `md:grid-cols-3` means "3-column grid on medium screens and above." This mobile-first approach lets you build responsive layouts entirely in HTML without media queries.
example:
```html
MyApp
Home About Contact
Card 1
Card 2
Card 3
Card 4

Centered Content

Dashboard Settings

Main Content

Page content goes here.

Header
Content
Footer
```
key_takeaway: Use `flex` + `items-center` + `justify-center` for centering. Use `grid` + `grid-cols-N` + `gap-N` for card layouts. Responsive prefixes (`md:grid-cols-3`) replace media queries.
try_it: Build a responsive dashboard layout with a sidebar (hidden on mobile, shown on desktop) and a main content area with a responsive card grid.

explanation:
Tailwind's responsive system uses mobile-first breakpoints: `sm:` (640px), `md:` (768px), `lg:` (1024px), `xl:` (1280px), `2xl:` (1536px). You write the mobile layout first (no prefix), then layer on larger-screen styles with prefixes. This is exactly the mobile-first CSS approach, but without leaving your HTML.

Every utility in Tailwind can be made responsive: `text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg` means small text on mobile, base on tablets, large on desktop. Container queries (`@container`) are also supported with `@` prefix utilities for component-level responsiveness.
example:
```html

Responsive Heading

This text scales smoothly across breakpoints.

Content with responsive padding.
Mobile only
Tablet only
Desktop only
Sidebar
Main

Title

Description...

Column 1
Column 2
```
key_takeaway: Mobile-first: write base styles for small screens, then add `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:` prefixes for larger screens. Every utility is responsive. Container queries (`@container`) enable component-level breakpoints.
try_it: Build a product card that shows as a horizontal row on desktop and a vertical stack on mobile using only Tailwind responsive utilities.

explanation:
Tailwind's utility classes can become verbose when repeated across many elements. The `@apply` directive extracts common patterns into reusable CSS classes. Component libraries like Headless UI and Radix provide accessible, unstyled components designed for Tailwind. Template literals in frameworks (React, Vue) enable component-level reuse.

The recommended pattern is to create small, focused components rather than large CSS abstractions. A `Button` component with variants (`primary`, `secondary`, `ghost`) encapsulates the styling logic. Tailwind's plugin system lets you add custom utilities for project-specific patterns.
key_takeaway: Use `@apply` to extract repeated utility patterns into component classes. Use React/Vue components with variant props for dynamic styling. Custom plugins add project-specific utilities.
try_it: Create a reusable `Button` component with `primary`, `secondary`, `danger`, and `ghost` variants, plus `sm`, `md`, `lg` sizes, using Tailwind utilities.

explanation:
Tailwind's `tailwind.config.js` customizes the entire design system — colors, spacing, fonts, breakpoints, and more. The `theme.extend` key adds new values without replacing defaults. The `content` array is critical — it tells Tailwind which files to scan for class usage, enabling tree-shaking of unused styles in production.

Dark mode, custom fonts, and design tokens are configured here. The `darkMode: 'class'` strategy uses a `.dark` class on `<html>` to toggle themes. CSS custom properties can bridge Tailwind's config with runtime theme switching.
key_takeaway: `tailwind.config.js` is the design system source of truth. Use `theme.extend` for custom colors, fonts, and animations. `darkMode: 'class'` enables manual dark mode toggling with `dark:` prefix utilities.
try_it: Configure a Tailwind project with custom brand colors, a display font, dark mode support, and a custom animation. Build a hero section that uses all of these.

explanation:
Tailwind CSS practice focuses on composing utility classes to build responsive, themed components without writing custom CSS. The core pattern is mobile-first responsive design: write base styles for small screens, layer on `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:` prefixes for larger screens. Every utility in Tailwind can be made responsive.

For component reuse, the pattern is: use `@layer components` with `@apply` for frequently repeated patterns, and framework components (React/Vue) with variant props for dynamic styling. Dark mode uses the `dark:` prefix with the class-based strategy. Master the config file to customize colors, fonts, and animations for your design system.
example:
```html

Choose Your Plan

Free

$0/mo

  • 5 projects
  • 1GB storage

Pro

$29/mo

```
key_takeaway: Tailwind practice: mobile-first responsive with breakpoints, dark: prefix for theming, @apply for reusable component classes, config for design system.
try_it: Complete all 5 problems. Each builds a different UI pattern with Tailwind utilities.

practice_challenges.title: Responsive Dashboard Layout
practice_challenges.hint: Use flex for sidebar + main, grid for card layout in main area. Hide sidebar on mobile with md:block/hidden.
practice_challenges.description: Build a responsive dashboard with a collapsible sidebar (hidden on mobile), a header with nav, and a main area with a responsive card grid (1 col mobile, 2 tablet, 4 desktop).
practice_challenges.example_output: Sidebar hidden, hamburger menu visible, cards stacked in single column, full-width header

practice_challenges.title: Dark Mode Toggle Component
practice_challenges.hint: Use darkMode: 'class' in config, toggle .dark on html element, apply dark:bg-* and dark:text-* utilities.
practice_challenges.description: Build a page with dark mode toggle. Include a navbar, hero section, feature cards, and footer. All elements must look good in both light and dark themes.
practice_challenges.example_output: Background switches from white to dark gray, text inverts, cards get dark backgrounds, images adjust brightness

practice_challenges.title: Animated Modal with Transitions
practice_challenges.hint: Define fadeIn/slideUp keyframes in config, use transition-all with duration-300, and backdrop-blur for overlay.
practice_challenges.description: Build a modal component with fade-in overlay, slide-up content, backdrop blur, and close button. Include form inputs styled with Tailwind's forms plugin.
practice_challenges.example_output: Overlay fades in with backdrop blur, modal slides up from bottom, focus trapped inside, Escape closes

practice_challenges.title: Responsive E-commerce Product Card
practice_challenges.hint: Extract card styles with @layer components and @apply. Use responsive grid for layout. Add hover effects with group-hover:.
practice_challenges.description: Build a product card component with image, title, price, rating stars, and add-to-cart button. Cards should be responsive and have hover effects.
practice_challenges.example_output: Card with image, truncated title, formatted price, star rating, 'Add to Cart' button. Hover: image zooms, shadow increases.

practice_challenges.title: Full Landing Page with Custom Theme
practice_challenges.hint: Extend config with brand colors, custom fonts (display/sans/mono), keyframe animations, and custom spacing. Use all in a complete landing page.
practice_challenges.description: Build a complete SaaS landing page: hero with gradient background, feature grid, pricing table, testimonials carousel, CTA section, and footer. Use custom theme with brand colors and display fonts.
practice_challenges.example_input: Brand: purple/indigo gradient, display font: Inter, max-width: 1280px
practice_challenges.example_output: Full landing page with smooth scroll, animated feature cards, responsive pricing grid, and consistent brand theming across all sections
